title: Missing nonce verification in form handlers
summary: Form handlers and AJAX endpoints should verify nonces to prevent CSRF attacks.

detection.notes: Heuristic check - may have false positives. Look for $_POST/$_REQUEST usage without nearby nonce checks.

guidance.why_it_matters: Without nonce verification, forms are vulnerable to Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF) attacks. Attackers can trick authenticated users into performing unintended actions.
guidance.fix: Add wp_nonce_field() to your forms and verify with wp_verify_nonce() or check_admin_referer() before processing.
guidance.false_positive_risk: Medium. The heuristic may flag legitimate read-only uses of $_POST. Review each occurrence.
examples: Verify in handler: if (!wp_verify_nonce($_POST['my_nonce'], 'my_action')) wp_die('Invalid nonce');
